epigraph.text: Prove all things; hold fast that which is good.
epigraph.quote: The purpose of the $t$-test is to determine whether a sample could have come from a given population.

content.text: We now apply the hypothesis testing framework to population means.

content.title: One-Sample $t$-Test
content.problem:
A sample of 25 light bulbs has mean lifetime $\xbar = 1030$ hours 
and $S = 120$ hours. Test if the mean exceeds 1000 at $\alpha = 0.05$.
content.solution:
$H_0: \mu = 1000$, $H_a: \mu > 1000$ (one-tailed).

$t = \frac{1030 - 1000}{120/\sqrt{25}} = \frac{30}{24} = 1.25$

$df = 24$, $p$-value $= P(t_{24} > 1.25) \approx 0.11$.

Since $0.11 > 0.05$, fail to reject $H_0$. Insufficient evidence that 
mean exceeds 1000 hours.

content.text:
A two-tailed test at level $\alpha$ rejects $H_0: \mu = \mu_0$ if and 
only if $\mu_0$ falls outside the $(1-\alpha)$ confidence interval.
